MA Credit Income Trust, an ASX-listed credit and income investment vehicle, offers investors access to a managed portfolio of credit assets designed to generate income and preserve capital. data.title: Li Ning Posts Modest Q1 2026 Sell-Through Growth Amid Store Network Optimization
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

An update from Li Ning Company ( (HK:2331) ) is now available.

Li Ning reported that retail sell-through for its LI-NING branded points of sale in China, excluding LI-NING YOUNG, grew by a mid-single-digit percentage year-on-year in the first quarter of 2026. Offline channels saw mid-single-digit growth, driven by low-teens gains in directly operated retail stores and a low-single-digit increase in franchised wholesale outlets, while e-commerce posted high-single-digit growth.

As of 31 March 2026, the number of LI-NING points of sale in China stood at 6,075, reflecting a modest net reduction of 16 locations since the start of the year as store rationalization in direct retail outweighed expansion in wholesale. The LI-NING YOUNG network shrank by 54 to 1,464 locations, underscoring a continued optimization of the store portfolio even as overall sell-through improves, and the company cautioned investors that the disclosed figures are unaudited operational data that may be adjusted.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:2331) stock is a Buy with a HK$26.70 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Li Ning Company stock, see the HK:2331 Stock Forecast page.

More about Li Ning Company

Li Ning Company Limited is a leading Chinese sportswear group focused on athletic apparel, footwear and related sporting goods. Listed in Hong Kong, it operates through a mix of directly operated retail stores, franchised wholesale outlets and e-commerce platforms, targeting mass-market and performance-oriented consumers under the LI-NING and LI-NING YOUNG brands.

Average Trading Volume: 23,005,384

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: HK$54.29B

data.description:

Anhui Jinyan Kaolin New Materials Co. Ltd. Class H ( (HK:2693) ) just unveiled an update.

Anhui Jinyan Kaolin New Materials Co., Ltd. held its 2025 annual general meeting on 21 April 2026 in Huaibei, Anhui Province, with shareholders and proxies representing about 79.18% of issued shares in attendance. The meeting was convened in compliance with PRC laws, Hong Kong Listing Rules and the company’s articles, with all directors present except one independent non-executive director absent for work reasons.

Shareholders unanimously approved all eight ordinary resolutions by poll, including the 2025 board work report, independent directors’ duty report, annual reports, re-appointment of the 2026 accounting firm, the 2025 profit distribution plan, banking facilities for 2026 and the 2026 directors’ remuneration plan. The results confirm strong shareholder support for the company’s governance, financial arrangements and operational plans, providing a stable mandate for its upcoming financial year activities.

More about Anhui Jinyan Kaolin New Materials Co. Ltd. Class H

Anhui Jinyan Kaolin New Materials Co., Ltd. is a People’s Republic of China-based joint stock company listed in Hong Kong, engaged in the kaolin new materials sector. The company issues both H shares and domestic shares, targeting investors in Hong Kong and the mainland, and operates under the regulatory framework of the Hong Kong Listing Rules and PRC corporate law.

The latest update is out from Sunrise Energy Metals ( (AU:SRL) ).

Sunrise Energy Metals has applied for quotation on the ASX of 21,260 new fully paid ordinary shares, to be traded under the existing SRL code. The shares, issued on 21 April 2026 following the exercise or conversion of existing options or other convertible securities, represent a small incremental increase in the company’s listed capital base, with limited immediate impact on its market position but signaling ongoing capital structure activity.

While the volume of new securities is modest, the move reflects continued engagement by holders of options or convertible instruments in Sunrise Energy Metals’ equity story. For existing shareholders and market observers, the additional shares marginally dilute holdings but may also be interpreted as a vote of confidence by investors choosing to convert into ordinary equity at this stage.

data.description:

OM Holdings Ltd. ( (AU:OMH) ) has shared an announcement.

OM Holdings Limited has lodged its updated corporate governance statement for the financial year ended 31 December 2025, confirming board approval as of 21 April 2026 and providing access via its annual report and corporate website. The filing, accompanied by a completed Appendix 4G, affirms that the company adheres to key ASX Corporate Governance Council principles, including a formal board charter, pre-appointment checks for directors and executives, written appointment agreements, and direct board accountability for the company secretary.

These disclosures are intended to help investors and regulators easily locate and verify the company’s governance practices in line with ASX Listing Rules. By demonstrating full compliance across core governance recommendations, OM Holdings underscores its commitment to transparency and robust oversight, which may support investor confidence and align the group with best-practice standards expected of ASX-listed entities.

More about OM Holdings Ltd.

OM Holdings Limited is an ASX-listed company that reports under the Australian corporate governance framework. The group operates with a formal board and management structure, supported by documented charters and written agreements with directors and senior executives, and positions itself as a compliant participant in regulated capital markets.

data.title: Alphamab’s KN026 Breast Cancer Trial Secures High-Profile ASCO 2026 Slot
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Alphamab Oncology ( (HK:9966) ) has issued an update.

Alphamab Oncology announced that phase III trial results for KN026, its anti-HER2 bispecific antibody co-developed with CSPC subsidiary JMT-Bio, in combination with albumin-bound docetaxel HB1801 as neoadjuvant therapy for HER2-positive breast cancer, have been selected for a Late-Breaking Abstract oral presentation at the 2026 ASCO Annual Meeting. The head-to-head study showed KN026 plus HB1801 significantly improved total pathological complete response versus the current standard regimen of trastuzumab, pertuzumab and docetaxel, suggesting the regimen could become a new standard of care and reinforcing Alphamab’s position in HER2-targeted oncology, while KN026 continues to advance through multiple registrational trials in breast and gastric cancers with regulatory designations in China and the U.S.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:9966) stock is a Buy with a HK$9.60 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Alphamab Oncology stock, see the HK:9966 Stock Forecast page.

More about Alphamab Oncology

Alphamab Oncology is a China-based biopharmaceutical company focused on developing innovative oncology therapies, including bispecific antibodies such as KN026. The company targets cancers driven by HER2 and other biomarkers, with a growing portfolio of late-stage clinical programs and partnerships that emphasize both domestic and international commercialization opportunities.

Average Trading Volume: 1,559,211

Technical Sentiment Signal: Strong Buy

Current Market Cap: HK$10.94B

data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Marvel Gold Ltd. ( (AU:MVL) ) has shared an announcement.

Marvel Gold Limited has convened its Annual General Meeting for shareholders to be held on 21 May 2026 at the company’s offices in Subiaco, Western Australia. The company will provide the Notice of Meeting primarily online, sending physical copies only to investors who have opted in, reflecting a continued shift toward digital communications in shareholder engagement.

Shareholders are strongly encouraged to vote in advance of the meeting by lodging proxy forms electronically, by mail, fax or mobile before the 19 May 2026 deadline, as late instructions will not be accepted. The emphasis on proxy voting underscores the company’s push for broad participation in governance matters, with investors advised to seek professional guidance if uncertain about how to vote on the AGM business.

More about Marvel Gold Ltd.

Marvel Gold Limited is an Australia-based resources company focused on gold exploration and development. The company operates within the mining and exploration sector and is listed for trading on the Australian market, with its shareholder base managed via Computershare Investor Services.

data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

European Lithium ( (AU:EUR) ) just unveiled an update.

European Lithium Limited has updated the market on changes to its capital structure, reporting the cessation of a class of performance rights listed under the ASX security code EURAJ. The company advised that 30,000,000 of these performance rights were cancelled on 21 April 2026 by mutual agreement between the entity and the holder, reducing the number of such rights on issue.

The cancellation of this substantial block of performance rights may streamline the company’s equity structure and potentially lessen future dilution for existing shareholders. It also signals a renegotiation or conclusion of prior incentive or performance-based arrangements, which could have implications for management incentives and the company’s approach to capital management going forward.

More about European Lithium

European Lithium Limited is an ASX-listed company operating in the lithium sector, focused on the exploration and development of lithium assets to supply the battery and electric vehicle markets. The company’s securities trade under the ticker EUR on the Australian Securities Exchange, reflecting its positioning within the broader battery materials and clean energy value chain.
